resume.rawText:
Fabio Bellini 
Keizer, Oregon 
503-999-0775 
info@fabiobellini.com 
linkedin.com/in/fabio-b-/ 
https://www.cyberabe.com 
Professional Summary 
As an OSOC Security Analyst and dedicated cybersecurity professional, I specialize in penetration 
testing, vulnerability assessment, and security risk mitigation. I bring a strong focus on protecting 
organizational assets through the design and implementation of comprehensive security measures. With 
hands-on experience in tools such as Kali Linux, Active Directory, and Python, I’m proficient in red team 
operations and network security auditing. 
My background includes serving as an assistant instructor, mentoring students through practical 
cybersecurity training programs. I’ve also completed personal projects—including building a custom 
penetration testing lab with Raspberry Pi and developing Arduino-based hardware solutions—that reflect 
my technical creativity and problem-solving skills. 
In addition to my technical expertise, I have a diverse professional background, having owned and 
managed an online retail business and held leadership roles in the hospitality industry. Fluent in English 
and Italian, I combine a global perspective with a passion for cybersecurity to deliver results that protect 
and strengthen digital infrastructures.  
Technical Skills 
Penetration Testing • Cybersecurity • Network Security • Active Directory • Red Team • Android • Kali 
Linux • HTML • Python Language • Microsoft 365 
Licenses & Certifications 
CNPen (Certified Network Pentester) - The SecOps Group 
eCPPT (Certified Professional Penetration Tester) - INE 
PNPT (Practical Network Penetration Tester) - TCM Security 
CompTIA Security+ CE Certification – CompTIA 
Certified Penetration Tester - Evolve Academy 
Education 
OSCP Bootcamp         Evolve Academy 
Certificate of Completion 
Active Defense & Cyber Deception | John Strand    Antisyphon Training  
SOC Core Skills | John Strand 
Red Team Fundamentals for Active Directory | Eric Kuehn  
Certificates of Completion 
API Security Fundamentals        APIsec University 
Certificate of Completion 
Practical Ethical Hacking        TCM Security 
Certificate of Completion 
Languages 
English - Professional / Italian - Native 

Personal Projects 
   
• Raspberry Pi (https://www.raspberrypi.com/products/) - Created a Penetration Tester Lab with 
Raspberry Pi 400 and 2 Raspberry Pi 4 Model B 4GB ram with the use of “Damn Vulnerable Web 
Application” (DVWA) and OWASP Juice Shop 
 
• Arduino (https://www.arduino.cc/) - Created a RFID Reader/Writer with Arduino Uno R3 model , Wi- Fi 
Jammer with the use of ESP8266 module and a temperature and humidity controller accessible through 
Arduino Cloud.  
• CTF on-line (https://ctftime.org/) - TryHackMe (top 1%) (https://tryhackme.com/) - Hack the Box 
(Hacker level) (https://www.hackthebox.com/) - Cryptohack (https:// cryptohack.org/) 
Professional Experience 
OSOC Security Analyst       May 2024 - present 
Evolve Security | OSOC (Offensive Security Operations Center) Security Analyst 
•Perform continuous penetration testing to proactively identify and exploit vulnerabilities across 
networks, web applications, and internal systems, helping clients maintain a strong security posture in 
real time. 
•Correlate findings with SOC threat intelligence to prioritize and escalate risks, ensuring rapid 
remediation of critical vulnerabilities. 
•Automate reconnaissance and exploitation workflows where possible to support efficient and 
consistent testing cycles. Provide regular reports and real-time alerts to stakeholders, enabling 
continuous risk awareness and compliance tracking. 
Assistant Instructor         Jan 2024 - present 
Evolve Academy | ESCP Cybersecurity Boot Camp 
•Guide students toward achieving learning objectives and ensure they understand the material  
and complete it within the assigned timeframe to the best of their ability. 
•Implement additional guidance, resources, and technical exercises into the course as needed.  
•Perform 1-on-1 weekly interviews with students to assess progress and office hours to support the 
group and the overall cohort.  
•Assist other assistants and lead instructors with assessments as needed.  
•Responsible for grading CYBR final exams to ensure grades are issued to students in the promised 
timeframe.  
Student                 present 
Evolve Academy |   
•focusing on cybersecurity by attending an OSCP (Offensive Security Certified Professional) Bootcamp 
with Evolve Academy.  
•Currently enrolled in OffSec PEN-200 and OSWE-300 in order to obtain the OSCP certification and 
OSWE certification. 
Additional Work Experience 
Owner                       Jan 2019 - Jan 2022  
Athletes Best Equipment | On-line Clothing Store 
Tasting Room Lead         May 2016 - Nov 2018  
Alexana Estate Vineyard & Winery 
 
Relocation to U.S.A. from Italy                   March 2015 
Head Sommelier - Ristorante La Torre 1* Michelin Star                                      Mar 2014 - Dec 2014 
COMO Castello Del Nero

1.question: In your most recent role, what part of the work felt most like you?
1.answer: The part that felt most like me was investigating problems, connecting technical details, and turning them into something clear and actionable. I enjoy digging into issues, understanding what is really happening, and then communicating that in a way others can use to make decisions.

2.question: What part of the role drained you fastest?
2.answer: What drained me fastest was repetitive work that required a lot of manual effort but did not involve much analysis, problem-solving, or improvement. I can do it when needed, but I am at my best when I am solving problems, finding patterns, and helping move work forward in a meaningful way.

3.question: What do others come to you for that isn’t necessarily in your job description?
3.answer: People often come to me to help make sense of technical situations, especially when something is unclear, urgent, or needs to be explained well. I am usually the person they ask when they need someone to investigate, organize the details, and turn complexity into a clear next step.

4.question: What type of challenge feels exciting rather than overwhelming?
4.answer: I find complex challenges exciting when they require investigation, critical thinking, and a structured approach. If there is a difficult problem with moving parts, and I can break it down, validate the details, and work toward a practical solution, that energizes me rather than overwhelms me.

5.question: Outside of work, what do you spend time practicing, improving, or maintaining? 2–4 bullets.
5.answer: I am best at work that sits at the intersection of analysis, problem-solving, and communication. I take complex or messy situations, figure out what matters, and turn that into something useful, whether that is a solution, a recommendation, or a clear path forward.

synthesis.patternSummary:
You don't just do the work — you set operating rules.

When something isn't working, you don't force it — you tighten constraints.

You notice, clarify, and sequence.
operateBest: Work where measures and incentives are consistent.
Work with explicit scope, ownership, and a stable cadence.
Work where dependencies can be made explicit and handoffs are defined.
loseEnergy: Work where cadence is missing and constraints reset each cycle.
Work where ownership is unclear and decisions have no owner.
Work where scope is unbounded and priorities shift without constraints.
